children.name: Platylophus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Jay, a medium-sized, crested passerine endemic to the Sundaic region of Southeast Asia. Its distinctive, elongated crest gives it a jay-like appearance, but molecular studies have confirmed it is not a true jay but an ancient lineage now placed in its own family. It is a shy, secretive bird of lowland rainforests.

children.name: Platylophus galericulatus
children.description: A medium-sized, dark brown bird with a tall, shaggy crest, a white throat patch, and a black-and-white banded tail, found in the lowland rainforests of Thailand, Malaysia, Sumatra, Java, and Borneo. It is a shy, skulking inhabitant of the forest understorey and mid-storey, foraging for insects and small vertebrates. It is threatened by deforestation across its fragmented range.
